chunks.text:
Course: EE 156 | EECS at UC Berkeley EE 156. Business and Finance for Engineers Catalog Description: Business and Finance for Engineers is a comprehensive course designed specifically for engineering students and others with a strong mathematical background. It provides a focused introduction to essential business and finance principles, distilling key elements of a traditional two-year MBA program into a curriculum tailored for technically-minded individuals. The course is particularly beneficial for students interested in starting their own businesses, and serves as an introduction for those interested in quantitative finance. Units: 3 Also Offered As: ELENG 156 Course Objectives:

Bridge Engineering and Business: Equip engineers with the knowledge to understand and navigate the financial and strategic aspects of organizations.

Enhance Communication: Enable effective communication with business professionals through an understanding of fundamental business language and concepts.

Strategic Contribution: Prepare students to contribute to high-level decision-making processes and potentially assume leadership roles within their organizations.

Entrepreneurial Finance Skills: Provide foundational knowledge for those interested in starting their own businesses.

Introduction to Quantitative Finance: Introduce the field of quantitative finance for students considering a career in this area.

Prerequisites: Engineering or STEM major. Exceptions made for others with strong mathematical background and permission from the instructor. Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: Yes Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
